complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Add drag-to-sort functionality for saved medications
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the drag-to-sort implementation into: 1) Install and configure vuedraggable-es library, 2) Add savePrescriptionOrder function to prescriptionStorage.ts with tests, 3) Modify PrescriptionList.vue to wrap list items with draggable component and add drag handles, 4) Add CSS styles for drag states (ghost, chosen, handle) with dark mode support, 5) Add unit tests for drag handle visibility and compare mode interaction. Consider edge cases: single prescription, compare mode active, localStorage quota exceeded.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This is a well-scoped feature with clear implementation path. The existing PrescriptionList.vue (165 lines) has a clean structure with v-for list rendering that can be wrapped with draggable. The prescriptionStorage.ts already has established patterns for localStorage operations and error handling (logging). The test files show mature testing patterns with proper mocking. Key factors reducing complexity: (1) vuedraggable-es is a mature library with straightforward Vue 3 integration, (2) The existing list structure uses v-for with key binding making draggable integration natural, (3) Storage layer already handles all CRUD operations - adding savePrescriptionOrder follows the same pattern as existing functions, (4) Compare mode already exists with checkbox visibility toggle - hiding drag handles follows the same pattern. Main implementation work: adding ~30 lines to component template/script, ~15 lines to storage module, ~50 lines of CSS, and ~40 lines of tests. No architectural changes required.
